sourceMovie.title: The Bad Guys
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Bad Guys (2022) stuck because it commits to its family premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Pierre Perifel's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The family and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bad Guys 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The family energy of The Bad Guys is alive in The Bad Guys 2 (2025), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Dog Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dog Man (2025) operates in the same family as The Bad Guys, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs (2009) belongs to the same conversation as The Bad Guys — both anchor their family in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Simpsons Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Bad Guys's family stayed with you, The Simpsons Movie (2007) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Shrek Forever After
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Bad Guys excels in family, Shrek Forever After (2010) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fantastic Mr. Fox (2009) is the natural next step after The Bad Guys: same family DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Boxtrolls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Bad Guys who respond to its family will find The Boxtrolls (2014) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Chicken Run: Dawn of the Nugget
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Bad Guys fans keep landing on Chicken Run: Dawn of the Nugget (2023) — the family is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Pooh's Heffalump Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Pooh's Heffalump Movie draws from the same family well as The Bad Guys, channeling comedy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2005 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Rugrats Go Wild
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Rugrats Go Wild (2003) captures the family appeal of The Bad Guys without simply imitating it. The comedy streak running through both films is what puts Rugrats Go Wild at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Bad Guys?
faq.answer: Try The Bad Guys 2, Dog Man, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs—each shares DNA with The Bad Guys while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Bad Guys?
faq.answer: The Bad Guys (2022) is best described as Family, Comedy, Crime, Animation, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Bad Guys part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Bad Guys Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
